title: Northrop Grumman (NOC)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Northrop Grumman. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what NOC is really worth.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
">
Based on historical multi-year revenue CAGR calculations from scraped data. We established a baseline growth rate that reflects Northrop Grumman's steady defense contract revenue streams without over-extrapolating transient surges.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
">
Based on historical multi-year revenue CAGR calculations from scraped data. We established a baseline growth rate that reflects Northrop Grumman's steady defense contract revenue streams without over-extrapolating transient surges.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
">
Set at 2.5%, representing long-term nominal GDP growth expectations. As a mature, apex defense contractor, NOC is expected to grow alongside the overall U.S. economy in perpetuity.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

items.title: Geopolitical Escalation
items.description: Increased global tensions or direct conflicts could lead to emergency defense spending bills, immediately boosting NOC's backlog and revenue growth beyond our 5% baseline.

items.title: Space Systems Outperformance
items.description: NOC is heavily invested in space systems. Outsized success in winning highly classified, lucrative space-based defense contracts could rapidly expand operating margins and FCF.

items.title: B-21 Raider Scaling
items.description: As the B-21 stealth bomber program transitions from low-rate initial production to full-scale deployment, economies of scale could yield higher profitability than currently modeled.

items.title: U.S. Budget Constraints
items.description: Political gridlock or a shift toward fiscal austerity in Washington could lead to defense budget freezes or cuts, stunting top-line growth.

items.title: Fixed-Price Contract Overruns
items.description: Defense contractors often face significant margin compression or outright losses on fixed-price development contracts if technological hurdles cause delays and cost overruns.

items.title: Supply Chain Fragility
items.description: Ongoing bottlenecks in acquiring specialized microelectronics, raw materials, or skilled labor could delay revenue recognition and compress FCF margins.

faq.question: Why use a 9.2% discount rate?
faq.answer: Compiled by Gemini 3.1. "Northrop Grumman (NOC) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, 2026.
